title: 続きを/voteで続けて。 • consensus mode の前提を確認します。まず Claude と GLM の live lane を起動できるかを確認し、同時に現在の auth- stale 導線設計を維持したまま continuation 可能かを見ます。 • Ran which glm └ glm not found • Explored └ Read agent-glm-general-reviewer.json, agent-claude-general-reviewer.json ────────────────────────────────────────────────...
summary_text: [phase:plan] [family:happy-kernel-completion] [provider_hint:codex] Findings: - `returnTo` is still an open-redirect risk. [happy-auth-gateway.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/domain/happy-auth-gateway.js) accepts any `returnTo` string, and [auth.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/auth/auth.js) redirects to it directly. This conflicts with the fail-closed condition that return targets stay inside the Happy app path. - The fallback path can still regress to Cockpit. [happy-auth-reentry.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/domain/happy-auth-reentry.js) still uses `liveSessionOpenUrl` as a reauth fallback when `authEntryUrl` is missing, so config drift would reintroduce the `/cockpit` punt the plan is trying to eliminate. - Coverage is helper-heavy, not flow-heavy. [test-auth-reentry.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-auth-reentry.js) and [test-auth-gateway.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-auth-gateway.js) validate pure functions, but there is no DOM/integration test for `auth.js` doing `password login -> token persist -> redirect -> Happy restore`. - `launchLiveSession` is now appending `returnTo`, which is good, but it does it by routing the live URL through the reauth URL builder in [app.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/app.js#L1003). That works for query stitching, but it mixes “auth entry” semantics with “live session open” semantics and is worth separating if more live-route branching gets added. Highest-value next fixes: - Restrict `returnTo` to same-app relative paths under `/happy` before any redirect. - Remove the Cockpit fallback from `buildHappyReauthPlan` once `./auth/` is the required route. - Add one integration test that exercises `auth.js` end-to-end with mocked `fetch`, `localStorage`, and redirect capture.
